value.prior_session_context: Tuesday 2026-06-23 saw a technology and semiconductor selloff; the S&P 500 fell 1.4% and the Nasdaq Composite fell 2.2%. June 24 was the first session after that rout, and the S&P 500 and Nasdaq closed lower for a third consecutive day.

text: Wednesday, June 24, 2026 was a regular trading day for US cash-equity and futures markets. The session followed Tuesday's global technology and semiconductor selloff, and resulted in a third consecutive decline for the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite. All referenced venues operated on normal Wednesday schedules; there were no market holidays.

sp_500.basis: AP close summary and CNBC close section; previous close 7,365.46

dow.basis: AP close summary; CNBC rounds to +0.35%; AP rounds to +0.4%

nasdaq_composite.basis: AP close summary and CNBC close section; CNBC -0.43% unrounded; previous close 25,587.04

text: US equity indexes closed mixed on Wednesday, June 24, 2026. The S&P 500 fell 7.24 points (-0.1%) to 7,358.22, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 182.06 points (+0.4%) to 51,848.90, the Nasdaq Composite fell 110.40 points (-0.4%) to 25,476.64, and the Russell 2000 rose 11.15 points (+0.4%) to 2,986.63. Week-to-date, the S&P 500 is down 1.9%, the Dow is up 0.6%, the Nasdaq is down 3.9%, and the Russell 2000 is up 0.2%. Year-to-date, the S&P 500 is up 7.5%, the Dow is up 7.9%, the Nasdaq is up 9.6%, and the Russell 2000 is up 20.3%.

value.sp_500_breadth: nearly 2 out of every 3 stocks gained ground despite the index falling 0.1%
value.interpretation: Positive internal breadth diverged from the cap-weighted index decline, indicating that the sell-off was concentrated in large-cap tech rather than broad-based
value.context: AP noted the same pattern on Tuesday: tech stocks pulled the market lower despite broader gains elsewhere

text: The S&P 500 fell 0.1% despite nearly 2 out of every 3 stocks in the index gaining ground, a positive breadth divergence indicating that the decline was driven by large-cap technology weights rather than broad selling. AP explicitly noted this pattern and highlighted that the same dynamic occurred on Tuesday, when tech stocks pulled the market lower despite broader gains.

microsoft.note: Heaviest weight on the market per AP

alphabet.note: Company replacing Verizon in the Dow on Monday; S&P Global announced the change Tuesday

value.context: AP framed these as losses for several influential tech heavyweights including Microsoft that weighed down the broader market even though most stocks gained

text: Large-cap technology stocks were the primary drag on the S&P 500. Microsoft fell 2.3% and was the heaviest weight on the market, Oracle slumped 4.6%, and Alphabet slipped 0.2%. AP noted that declines for several influential tech heavyweights pulled the broader market lower despite positive internal breadth. Alphabet is set to replace Verizon in the Dow Jones Industrial Average on Monday, becoming the fifth Magnificent 7 company in the price-weighted index.

brent_crude.discrepancy_note: AP and CNBC/Reuters cite different settlement levels for Brent on June 24: AP reports $73.87 (-3.8%), while CNBC reports $73.74 (-4.33%). The discrepancy reflects vendor rounding and timing differences in settlement reporting.

wti_crude.note: AP and CNBC agree on the $70.34 settle

value.context: Brent at its lowest level since before the US and Israel launched airstrikes against Iran at the end of February; WTI at its lowest since early March

energy_equities.cnbc_group_note: Exxon Mobil, Chevron, ConocoPhillips and SLB all declined more than 2%; XLE ETF down more than 1%

market_session: NYMEX/ICE regular settlement and US equity close, Wednesday 2026-06-24
text: Oil prices continued their decline as the US and Iran negotiated a possible end to their war. Brent crude settled at its lowest level since before the conflict began: AP reported $73.87 per barrel (-3.8%) while CNBC reported $73.74 (-4.33%), a vendor discrepancy disclosed above. US crude fell to $70.34 (-3.9% per AP, -3.92% per CNBC). Energy equities fell broadly: Exxon Mobil lost 2% and Chevron lost 2.6%, with CNBC reporting ConocoPhillips and SLB also down more than 2% and the XLE energy ETF down more than 1%.

ten_year.note: AP close: 10-year fell to 4.40% from 4.50% late Tuesday; CNBC noted it dropped below 4.5%

value.context: Yields remain elevated from earlier in the year; the 2-year more closely tracks Fed rate expectations; CME FedWatch shows at least one rate hike priced by December

text: Treasury yields mostly fell. The 10-year Treasury yield dropped to 4.40% from 4.50% late Tuesday, and the 2-year yield eased to 4.15% from 4.16%. Despite the decline, yields remain elevated from earlier in the year, especially the 2-year which tracks Fed rate expectations. CME Group data shows Wall Street forecasting at least one rate hike by December.

micron.note: CNBC: shares came off their lows but ended down 0.3% ahead of earnings after the bell; stock ended Tuesday at $1,051.77

sandisk.note: Both Micron and Sandisk tumbled 13% in the previous session

value.smh_etf: VanEck Semiconductor ETF ended marginally lower

micron_earnings_consensus.note: Analysts polled by FactSet see earnings of $20.83 per share on revenue of $35.75 billion

text: Chip and memory stocks stabilized somewhat after Tuesday's rout but still closed lower. Micron Technology ended down 0.3%, coming off its session lows ahead of its fiscal Q3 earnings release after the bell. Analysts polled by FactSet see earnings of $20.83 per share on revenue of $35.75 billion. Fellow memory stock Sandisk dropped 2.5%. Both had tumbled 13% on Tuesday. The VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) ended marginally lower.

homebuilders.note: AP: surged 16.7% after revenue beat; CNBC: up more than 17% intraday, best day since Jan 2022
homebuilders.driver: Approval of legislation beneficial to the homebuilder industry

gold.note: AP close: gold fell 3.4% to settle at $4,008.80; briefly traded below $4,000 intraday, lowest since November

pce_preview.note: Economists expect PCE prices rose 4.1% in May, highest in three years; core expected 3.4% per Investopedia/Dow Jones Newswires survey
pce_preview.context: Fed signaled considering a rate hike by year-end; CME FedWatch shows at least one hike priced by December

text: Homebuilders were among the biggest winners: KB Home surged 16.7% and D.R. Horton jumped 6.7% following approval of legislation beneficial to the industry. Gold fell 3.4% to settle at $4,008.80 an ounce, briefly trading below $4,000 for the first time since November. Investors are now focused on Thursday's PCE inflation report, with economists expecting prices to have risen 4.1% in May, which would be the highest level in three years. Fed Chair Kevin Warsh emphasized the central bank's commitment to price stability in last week's meeting.
